name: ExtensionObservationDefinition_Specimen
title: R5: Kind of specimen used by this type of observation (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ObservationDefinition.specimen` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ObservationDefinition.specimen` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/SpecimenDefinition)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ObservationDefinition.specimen` 0..* `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/SpecimenDefinition)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ObservationDefinition.specimen` has a context of ObservationDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ObservationDefinition`.
Element `ObservationDefinition.specimen` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Kind of specimen used by this type of observation (new)
element.comment:
Element `ObservationDefinition.specimen` has a context of ObservationDefinition based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ObservationDefinition`.
Element `ObservationDefinition.specimen` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Only used for in vitro observations. When multiple occurrences of specimen are present, they are to be combined with a logical AND: all specified specimens  are needed for the observation.

element.short: Kind of specimen used by this type of observation
element.definition: The kind of specimen that this type of observation is produced on.
element.comment: Only used for in vitro observations. When multiple occurrences of specimen are present, they are to be combined with a logical AND: all specified specimens  are needed for the observation.
